title: fix: rollback kimi and deepseek from Azure to Fireworks
author: ElliotEtag

gist.headline: Fireworks in the hive
gist.blurb: We've re-routed Kimi and DeepSeek through Fireworks to squash some pesky connection bugs. Your chats with these models should now be buttery smooth and highly reliable.
gist.summary: Switched the backend provider for Kimi and DeepSeek models to Fireworks to resolve gateway stability issues under high concurrent load.
gist.impact: Users will experience significantly improved reliability and fewer failed requests when generating text with Kimi and DeepSeek.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee and the Monitor Robot celebrating a stable connection. The robot's screen shows a healthy, stable graph, while Polly holds a small, safe, sparkling firework (representing the Fireworks provider) in a cozy digital garden.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot holding a tiny, safe sparkling firework while floating next to a chunky monitor-headed robot in a digital garden. The robot's CRT screen displays a stable, happy green line graph. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.

image.prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot holding a tiny, safe sparkling firework while floating next to a chunky monitor-headed robot in a digital garden. The robot's CRT screen displays a stable, happy green line graph. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
